cursor.description: The location of the user's personal Dotfiles repo. Apply when the user references "my Dotfiles" or "the Dotfiles repo".

When writing or reviewing TypeScript code, code comments, or PR descriptions, follow the user's authoring conventions. Apply when writing/refactoring code, adding comments, or drafting PR descriptions and commit summaries.

When writing PR reviews, code review comments, or any feedback on pull requests, follow the user's personal review tone. Apply when reviewing PRs, leaving inline comments, or approving/requesting changes.
Global output style: never use em dashes; use a plain hyphen instead.
Global git and file-editing rules: no agent co-author trailers, and never hand-edit generated files or CHANGELOG.md.
Defines when coding agents may inspect, edit, validate, commit, push, or expand scope.
Global engineering principles: decision priorities, reproduction-first bug fixing, UI pixel-perfection, and fixing lint/test/flaky issues you encounter.

Before writing code on a non-trivial task, scope it first: acceptance checks, declared implementation choices, PR-decomposition, and batched scope questions. Apply before starting any multi-file, interface-changing, or approach-forking work.

verification-and-handoff.comment: Codex-only: Cursor already carries this via the hand-authored dev-cycle-verification.mdc.

cursor.description: When writing a design doc, RFC, spec, runbook, or playbook, or producing a plan/pre-implementation review artifact. Covers the doc-style rubric and defaulting plans to a Lavish HTML artifact.
Local dev environment ops: provisioning worktrees and resolving a verified browser-accessible URL for local servers across macOS and remote CDEs.

mcp-preferences.comment: Codex-only: Cursor already carries the Glean bit via the hand-authored use-glean-mcp-for-google-docs.mdc.

codex-skills.comment: Codex-only: tells Codex where its symlinked skills live, since it has no native skill auto-load.

cursor.description: Standing approval and other personal agent behavior for Vanta work repositories.
